OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a UX designer who spends several hours daily on social media, I see firsthand how fake content can distort reality and spread misinformation. My low openness score means I prefer familiar information, which makes the influx of unverified claims especially unsettling. To manage this, I rely on fact-checking tools and cross-referencing content with credible sources. I also curate my feeds to follow verified accounts and organizations that prioritize accuracy. Being in a relationship and a parent, I'm acutely aware of the impact such content can have on my family, which drives my proactive approach to identifying and reporting fake content. My high neuroticism level makes me more sensitive to the anxiety-inducing effects of misinformation, so maintaining mental well-being is crucial. I use mindfulness techniques to balance my consumption and avoid信息过载。Hiking and baking help me unwind and stay grounded, which in turn sharpens my ability to discern credible information from fake news.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona's background as a UX designer in New York, coupled with their high neuroticism and low openness, influences their approach to managing fake content. They use fact-checking tools and curate their social media feeds to reduce anxiety. Their hobbies provide emotional grounding, enhancing their ability to discern accurate information.
response_parsed.tone: Reflective and cautious, with a focus on personal well-being and proactive measures.
